rulings.Case Title: WHEELER SUPPORTIVE SERVICES, INC. VS. GOLDEN GATE REGIONAL CENTER, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 28, 2015, Line 14, PETITIONER WHEELER SUPPORTIVE SERVICES, INC. Hearing For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ Of Mandate. Petition denied. The court denies the petition for writ of mandate and damages because the language in 17 CCR § 58660 (a) does not impose a duty on the regional center to initiate contractual negotiations with a specific vendor or to accept a vendor's request to negotiate a rate. The language 17 CCR § 58660 (b) clearly provides that it is the regional center's decision to negotiate with a particular vendor for the services outlined Title 17, Section 54349 (a) through (d), and specified in Section 58614 (b). Petitioner's failure to prove that it has a right to contract with Golden Gate Regional Center precludes its claim for contract damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JOSHUA WILLIAMS et al VS. SWISSPORT NORTH AMERICA, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORAT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Electronic Timekeeping And Payroll Records
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, September 28, 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS JOSHUA WILLIAMS, ROGER VALLE'S Motion To Compel Electronic Timekeeping And Payroll Records. Pro Tem Judge Philip Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.anderson.nx3z@statefarm.com ,a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NICK C. GRAY et al VS. MARGARET L. NARRON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 28, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT MARGARET NARRON MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The anti-SLAPP motion to strike is denied. The motion is denied as moot as to the negligence cause of action because plaintiff dismissed that claim long before defendant filed this motion. The motion is denied as to the malicious prosecution cause of action. Defendant has met the first prong of CCP 425.16 and shown that the malicious prosecution claim arises from petitioning activity. Plaintiffs have met the second prong and shown a probability of prevailing on the merits. "The determination of the reasons underlying the dismissal is a question of fact." Haight v. Handweiler (1988) 199 Cal.App.3d 85. Here, it is not clear that the dismissal was based on a lack of standing. As the Court of Appeal found, it could equally be based on a failure to prosecute, which is a termination on the merits. See Minasian v. Sapse (1978) 80 Cal.App.3d 823, 827. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: HVCH LP, VS. PACIFIC INSTITUTE, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 28, 2015, Line 13, DEFENDANT NADER SHABAHANGI, PACIFIC INSTITUTE, AGESONG, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted in part, denied in part. Defendants' motion to strike Plaintiff's request for a permanent injunction is granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to establish why money damages are an inadequate remedy. If Plaintiff prevails in this lawsuit, the final judgment will be conclusive and enforceable by Plaintiff, rendering the need for an injunction unnecessary. (CCP 577, 1908 subd. (a)(2).) Defendants' motion to strike Plaintiff's request for foreclosure of deeds of trust and the appointment of a receiver is denied as moot as to the cause of action for foreclosure and as to the cause of action for specific performance in light of the ruling on Defendants' demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: HVCH LP, VS. PACIFIC INSTITUTE, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 28, 2015, Line 13, DEFENDANT NADER SHABAHANGI, PACIFIC INSTITUTE, AGESONG, INC. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained in part, denied in part. Defendants' demurrer is sustained as to the eighth cause of action for foreclosure and as to the ninth cause for specific performance without leave to amend. Plaintiff does not oppose the demurrer to the eighth and ninth causes of action on the basis of the standstill provision. Defendants' demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of written contract is denied. Plaintiff's second amended complaint is not a sham pleading. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to allege a material breach of contract and contractual damages. (Complaint 13(b-d and f-h) and 18-20.) Defendants' demurrer to Plaintiff's second cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is denied because Plaintiff's allegations are clearly made against Defendant Nader and not Synergy LLC. (Complaint 22-24.) Defendants' demurrer to the fifth and seventh causes of action is denied because Plaintiff has sufficiently pled in the complaint the capacities in which Nader was acting when he made the alleged negligent misrepresentations and engaged in the alleged negligent interference with protective economic relations. (Complaint 12 and 13.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: EDWARD ONG ET AL VS. TIE JUN LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint _1087231-01
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 28, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT TIE LIU, T J CONSTRUCTION INC., INTERNATIONAL FIDELITY INSURANCE COMPANY, SURETEC INSURANCE COMPANY, BUSINESS ALLIANCE INSURANCE COMPANY Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint. Defendants' Tie Jun Liu et al.'s Motion to Strike First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. Plaintiffs have failed to plead sufficient facts to support a showing of malice, oppression, or fraud on the part of Defendants. Plaintiffs also failed to plead the existence of a contract term of specific statute to recover attorney's fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: EDWARD ONG ET AL VS. TIE JUN LIU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 28, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT TIE LIU, T J CONSTRUCTION INC., INTERNATIONAL FIDELITY INSURANCE COMPANY, SURETEC INSURANCE COMPANY, BUSINESS ALLIANCE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' Tie Jun Liu et al.'s Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED IN PART. Plaintiffs do not state with specificity their causes of action for fraud and elder abuse. (See Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 216, held: " '[A]llegations of fraud involve a serious attack on character, and fairness to the defendant demands that he should receive the fullest possible details of the charge in order to prepare his defense.' ") Plaintiffs sufficiently plead their causes of action for negligence, breach of contract and unfair business practices. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
